skill.name: agent-tools
skill.description: Run 150+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation. Models: FLUX, Veo, Gemini, Grok, Claude, Seedance, OmniHuman, Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter, and more.
skill.summary: Execute cloud-based AI workflows through inference.sh CLI for images, video, LLMs, search, and automation without local GPU.
skill.author: inference-sh-7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 194 patterns, but most are false positives from documentation files. External command detections are markdown code blocks showing CLI examples, not executable code. Network URLs are documentation links. Environment variable access (INFSH_API_KEY) and filesystem paths (~/.local/bin) are standard for CLI tools. The curl|sh install pattern is common for CLI distribution. One medium concern: CLI executes external processes via inference.sh API, requiring user trust in the platform.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Dependency
medium_findings.description: Skill relies on external inference.sh CLI binary that executes cloud-based AI workloads. User must trust the inference.sh platform with API credentials and data sent for processing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill wraps the inference.sh CLI which sends data to external servers for processing - standard for cloud AI but requires user awareness.

low_findings.title: Standard CLI Installation Pattern
low_findings.description: Uses curl|sh installation method which is common but requires trusting the remote script source.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard pattern for CLI tools, but users should be aware they are executing remote code.

content.user_title: Run 150+ AI Apps via inference.sh CLI
content.value_statement: Access cloud-based AI models for image generation, video creation, LLMs, and search without local GPU setup. Execute inference.sh workflows directly through Claude Code using simple CLI commands.
actual_capabilities: Generate images using FLUX, Gemini, Grok, and Seedream models
Create videos with Veo, Seedance, Wan, and OmniHuman
Call LLMs including Claude, Gemini, Kimi, and GLM via OpenRouter
Perform web search through Tavily and Exa APIs
Automate Twitter/X posts, DMs, likes, and follows
List, search, and discover 150+ AI apps
Track async task status and retrieve results
limitations: Requires inference.sh account and API credentials
All processing happens in the cloud - not suitable for sensitive data
Usage subject to inference.sh quota and pricing
Requires internet connection for all operations

use_cases.title: AI Content Creation
use_cases.description: Generate marketing images, product videos, and 3D models on-demand without local GPU. Create multiple variations quickly for A/B testing.

use_cases.title: Rapid AI Prototyping
use_cases.description: Test different AI models (FLUX vs Gemini, Veo vs Seedance) for your use case before committing to a specific provider or self-hosting.

use_cases.title: Automated Workflows
use_cases.description: Build automated pipelines that generate social media content, transcribe audio, or search the web as part of larger AI agent workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image using FLUX with this prompt: "a futuristic city at sunset, cyberpunk style"

prompt_templates.title: Video Creation with Veo
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 5-second video using Veo 3.1 Fast showing "ocean waves crashing on rocky cliffs at sunrise"

prompt_templates.title: Web Search Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for the latest news about "quantum computing breakthroughs 2025" using Tavily and summarize the top 3 results

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Model Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate the same image prompt "a serene mountain landscape" using both FLUX and Gemini 3 Pro, then compare the results side by side

output_examples.input: Generate an image of a cat astronaut
Create a video of a drone flying over mountains

best_practices: Use --save flag to save sample inputs as templates for repeated workflows
Check app details with infsh app get before running to understand required inputs
Use --no-wait for long-running video generation tasks to avoid blocking
anti_patterns: Do not send sensitive or private data to cloud AI models
Avoid running apps without checking input schema first
Do not store API keys in code - use environment variables

faq.question: Do I need an inference.sh account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. Run infsh login to authenticate. The skill requires valid API credentials to execute AI workloads.

faq.question: Is my data private when using inference.sh?
faq.answer: All processing happens on inference.sh servers. Do not use for sensitive data. Check their privacy policy for data handling details.

faq.question: How does pricing work?
faq.answer: inference.sh uses credits-based pricing. Different models cost different amounts per generation. Check your balance with infsh me.

faq.question: Can I use this skill offline?
faq.answer: No. All AI inference happens in the cloud. An internet connection is required for all operations.

faq.question: What happens if a task fails?
faq.answer: The CLI returns an error message with details. Failed tasks are not charged to your account.

faq.question: Can I deploy my own AI models to inference.sh?
faq.answer: Yes. Use infsh app init and infsh app deploy to create and deploy custom AI workflows to the platform.
